title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Best Butter Chicken Recipe - Classic Murgh Makhani
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making the best Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) at home with our classic Indian recipe. Creamy, rich, and bursting with authentic flavors. Get the step-by-step guide!
h2Headings: How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for the Best Homemade Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A creamy, rich bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our Classic Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani – a true Indian delight!

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian dish celebrated for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. Tender pieces of marinated chicken are simmered in this luscious gravy, creating a symphony of mild spices and buttery notes. This classic recipe promises an authentic taste of India, perfect for a comforting and flavorful meal.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chili powder, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for best flavor. This step accounts for the bulk of the prep time.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 8 minutes, turning occasionally, until lightly browned and mostly cooked through. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5 minutes. Add the second tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the tomato puree or crushed tomatoes. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the raw tomato smell is gone. This step is crucial for deep flavor.

instructions.title: Blend and Refine the Gravy
instructions.text: Add the soaked cashews to the tomato mixture. Carefully transfer the hot sauce to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water if it's too thick. For an extra smooth restaurant-style gravy, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken pieces to the smooth gravy. Stir in the heavy cream, crushed Kasuri Methi, and sugar or honey. Simmer for another 7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce. Adjust salt and sweetness to taste. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

tips: For a truly smooth and rich gravy, always blend your tomato-onion base and then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve. This removes any fibrous bits for a restaurant-quality texture.
Kashmiri red chili powder provides vibrant color without excessive heat. For more spice, add a pinch of regular red chili powder or a slit green chili along with the tomatoes.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day as flavors meld and deepen. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheat gently on the stovetop.
article: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
